steps.title: Use complementary angles
steps.content: In a right triangle, the two acute angles are complementary. Since angle $M$ and angle $N$ add to $90^\circ$, the sine of one equals the cosine of the other.

steps.title: Transfer the trig value
steps.content: So $$ \sin(M)=\cos(N) $$ The problem gives $$ \sin(M)=\frac{\sqrt{35}}{6} $$ Therefore, $\cos(N)=\frac{\sqrt{35}}{6}$. The answer is B.
